codex_response:
Implemented `b942fd60` as a new ARC2 task package. The rule used is a recursive red path-growth process from the single `2`: lines run through zeros, stop at nonzero blockers, and create perpendicular turns with obstacle-claim logic matching the official examples. `arc2_sonnet45_summary.jsonl` was discarded as wrong; `arc2_opus46_summary.json` was a useful starting point but needed refinement for the official edge cases. No new shared primitive was added.

Validation passed end-to-end: official examples `7/7`, generated examples `1000/1000`. I built the artifacts and visually checked the preview sheets; the generated set looks on-family, with sparse singleton distractors and recursive orthogonal red expansions.
